questions.question: list.append(x) vs list.insert(0, x):
choices: append adds to end, insert adds at index 0
questions.explanation: append always adds to the end. insert(index, item) places the item at the specified index.

questions.question: "hello world".split() produces:
questions.explanation: split() without arguments splits on any whitespace and returns a list.

questions.question: a = [1,2,3]; a[1] = 10; what is a?
questions.explanation: List indices are zero-based. a[1] is the second element (2). Setting it to 10 gives [1, 10, 3].

questions.question: "abcd"[::-1] equals:
questions.explanation: [::-1] reverses the string. abc d reversed is dcba. A common Python idiom.
